title: peritoma multicaulis
intro: Peritoma multicaulis, also known as Bush sunflower, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of terpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Peritoma multicaulis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
